original: Felix Baumgartner, Austrian daredevil and the first skydiver to break the sound barrier, born in Salzburg, Austria (d. 2025)[1]

SpaceX’s Starship rocket - the most powerful ever made with 33 booster engines, launches in historic first test flight from Boca Chica, Texas.  Explodes four minutes into flight with blame centering onElon Musk's rush to launch on 420
NASA engineers successfully repair and recode Voyager 1 from 15 billion miles away after five month gap in not receiving data
Michael Jordansets NBA playoff record with 63 points in a game
US PresidentJimmy Carteris attacked by a swamp rabbit that swims up to his fishing boat in Plains, Georgia
NASA Apollo 16's John Young & Charles Duke land on the moon with Boeing Lunar Rover #2
NASA civilian pilotNeil Armstrongtakes X-15 to 63,250 m
Danica Patrick driving for Andretti Green Racing wins the Indy Japan 300 at Twin Ring Motegi; first female driver in history to win an IndyCar Series race
Total solar eclipse in Exeter, Western Australia blocks out the sun for 58 seconds
Marie and Pierre Curieisolate the radioactive compound radium chloride
Uranus passes Neptune (once every 171 years)
1st electron microscope demonstrated (RCA), Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
First pasteurization test is completed by FrenchmenLouis Pasteurand Claude Bernard
Chicago Bulls win record 72 games in a season
Andy Serkis, English actor (Gollum in Lord of the Rings), born in London, England
US Surveyor 3 lands on the Moon
Price of US oil turns negative for the 1st time in history - West Texas Intermediate, the benchmark for US oil, falls as low as minus $37.63 a barrel as worldwide demand falls
George Stibitz, American mathematician and scientist (father of the modern digital computer), born in York, Pennsylvania (d. 1995)[1]
Karyn Marshall of NYC lifted 303 lbs in a clean & jerk lift
Carlos Lopes runs world record marathon (2:07:12)
Mexican court bars sales of controversialFrida KahloBarbie doll
Stockholm experiences the atmospheric optical phenomenon called a Sun Dog or parhelion over the city, prompting omens against KingGustav Vasa
Eighth recorded perihelion passage of Halley's Comet
Halley's Comet passes 29th recorded perihelion at 87.9 million km
Soyuz T-8 launched; mission aborted when capsule fails to dock (lands 2 days later)
George Harrisonsings "The Lumberjack Song" withMonty Pythonin NYC
Only documented performance ofWilliam Shakespeare's tragedy "Macbeth" during the playwright's lifetime, at the Globe Theatre, London, in notes reviewing the play by astrologer Simon Forman
Walt Disney's animated film anthology "Make Mine Music" premieres in New York City
David Richmond, American civil rights activist (Greensboro Four lunch counter sit-in, 1960), born in Greensboro, North Carolina (d. 1990)
British rock band billed as "Roundabout" debuts in Kastrup, Denmark; after a brief tour of Scandinavia, they change their name to "Deep Purple"
Tito Puente, American mambo and Latin jazz bandleader ("Oye Como Va"), born in Manhattan, New York (d. 2000)
Svante Pääbo, Swedish geneticist (Nobel Prize for Physiology or Medicine 2022 - work on genomes of extinct hominins), born in Stockholm, Sweden[1]
William Bartram, American naturalist and writer (1st naturalist to document tropical forests of Florida in "Bartram's Travels"), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 1823)[1]
Chris Jarvis, English children's TV personality, born in Romford, London
Peter S. Beagle, American novelist and screenwriter (The Last Unicorn), born in New York City
Harold Lloyd, American silent and sound film actor, comedian, and director (Why Worry?; The Freshman; Safety Last!; The Sin of Harold Diddlebock), born in Burchard, Nebraska (d. 1971)
Lionel Hampton, American jazz vibraphone player, pianist, drummer, and actor, born in Louisville, Kentucky (d. 2002)
Comet C/1702 H1 approaches within 0.0437 AUs of Earth
Pakistani cricket batsmen Sohail & Inzamam make world record ODI partnership of 263 against NZ in Sharjah
All six EPL clubs withdraw from the controversial European Super League just 3 days after it was announced - Chelsea, Manchester City, Liverpool, Arsenal, Manchester United and Tottenham
Space shuttle STS-59 (Endeavour 6), lands
First mobile home (horse-drawn) used in a journey from London to Cyprus
Napoleon Bonapartebids emotional farewell to his old guard at Palace of Fontainebleau
French explorerJacques Cartiersets sail with two ships from Saint-Malo for Newfoundland, tasked by Francis I to look for gold and riches
Frenchman René Caillié is the first non-Muslim to enter Timbuktu and returns to win a 10,000 franc prize from the Société de Géographie

French mathematician Pierre Louis Maupertuis begins Lapland expedition to measure latitude and shape of the earth, joined by fellow scientistsAnders Celsius, Charles Etienne Louis Camus, Alexis Clairaut, and Pierre-Charles Le Monnier
193 people are killed and 11,826 are injured after a magnitude 7.0 earthquake strikes Lushan County, China
Four Christian ships break the siege of Constantinople and enter the Golden Horn lifting the morale of the defenders and embarrassingSultan Mehmed II
George Frideric Handelis buried in Westminster Abbey, London
8 2/3 inning perfect game pitched by Brian Holman of Oakland A's is spoiled by a home run hit by Ken Philips
Ted Williams' first MLB hit comes off of NY Yankees fellow future HOF'erRed Ruffingin Boston's 2-0 opening day defeat at Yankee Stadium, NYC
First baseball game in Hawaii, Cardinals beat Padres in doubleheader
Stanley Cup Final, Boston Garden, Boston, MA: Montreal Canadiens' 3-peat; beat Boston Bruins, 5-3 for a 4-2 for series win
Viacheslav Fetisov, Russian Hockey Hall of Fame defenseman (Olympic gold 1984, 88; Stanley Cup 1997-98, Detroit Red Wings), born in Moscow, Russia
Debbie Flintoff-King, Australian athlete (Olympic gold 400m hurdles 1988), born in Melbourne, Australia
Phil Hill, American auto racer (F1 world champion 1961; 24 Hours of Le Mans 1958, 61, 62), born in Miami, Florida (d. 2008)
First non-stop flight from Schiphol to Flamingo Airport, Bonaire in the Caribbean
Canadian ANIK A2 becomes 1st commercial satellite in orbit
Expo '92 opens in Seville Spain
The last three cruise ships still afloat amid COVID pandemic, finally dock at the ports of Marseille, France; Barcelona, Spain; and Los Angeles California
